response.justification: Candidate has broad full-stack experience and meaningful geospatial exposure through GISCollective and Green Map, but the role explicitly requires modern C++ (or Rust) for a routing data pipeline, and the resume shows only a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments over a decade ago. Recent work is dominated by TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, with no demonstrated experience in routing tiles, navigation guidance, or large-scale distributed pipeline systems. The gap between the required core stack and the candidate's recent trajectory is too wide for a mid-senior IC role focused on critical pipeline components.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial/GIS platform experience via GISCollective and Green Map
Low-latency systems background at Sociomantic (async I/O, high-throughput services)
Some prior C/C++ experience at National Instruments on production tooling
concerns: No modern C++ experience in roughly the last 10 years and no Rust experience
No navigation, routing, or map-tile pipeline experience
Recent stack (TS/Node, DLang) does not align with the required core technologies

key_factors.factor: Lack of recent modern C++ / Rust
No navigation or routing-tile pipeline experience
